Data.Description: Start a job for a process. Returns the created job(s) with keys. The <process-key> is the GUID from 'processes list' (not 'packages list'). Folder is optional — if not provided, it is inferred from the process. Use 'packages entry-points' to see input/output argument schemas for the process.

Arguments.Name: process-key
Arguments.Description: Process key (GUID) from 'processes list'. Identifies which process to run.

Options.Description: Tenant (optional, defaults to value selected during auth)
Folder path (optional — inferred from process if not provided)
Folder key (optional — inferred from process if not provided)
Execution strategy (ModernJobsCount, All, Specific, JobsCount)
Number of jobs to create (for JobsCount/ModernJobsCount strategy)
Input arguments as JSON string (mutually exclusive with --input-file). Auto-offloads to InputFile if >10K chars. Use 'packages entry-points' to see the expected schema.
Upload a file as the job's InputFile (mutually exclusive with --input-arguments). The file is uploaded as an attachment and its ID is set on the InputFile field.
Upload a file and attach it to the job (repeatable). Format: path (auto-names from filename) or name=path (explicit name). Example: --attachment ./doc.pdf --attachment invoice=./report.pdf
Attach an existing attachment by ID (repeatable). Use 'attachments list --job-key <key>' to find IDs from a previous job.
Execution runtime (Unattended, Headless, Serverless, NonProduction, Development, TestAutomation). Defaults to folder configuration.
Comma-separated user keys (GUIDs) to run as. Use 'users list' to find keys.
Comma-separated machine keys (GUIDs) to target. Use 'machines list' to find keys.
Environment variables as JSON string
Run job under the caller's identity
Job priority (Low, Normal, High)
User-specified reference for the job
Poll until the job reaches a terminal state (Successful, Faulted, Stopped) and return its output
Maximum time to wait for completion in seconds (default: 300)
Seconds between status checks (default: 5)
Directory for downloading OutputFile (default: current directory). Only with --wait-for-completion.
Skip automatic OutputFile download. Only with --wait-for-completion.
display help for command

Examples.Description: Start a process by its key

GlobalOptions.Description: Write logs to file instead of stderr
